activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of History and sources of historical information
activities.learning_activity: Describe the concept of History and the sources of historical information
suggested_methods: brainstorming
group discussion
jigsaw
resourceful person
study tour
self-reflection
activities.assessment_criteria: The concept of History and sources of Historical information is clearly described
suggested_resources: On-line materials and journal articles on the concept of History and historical charts, graphs, time-line on the sources of historical information

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of theories of the origin of human beings
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe the theories of the origin of human beings
suggested_methods: Invite religious leaders and historian/archaeologist to present on the origin of human beings.
Guide students to write a report on the theories and present for discussions
activities.assessment_criteria: Theories of the origin of human beings are correctly described
suggested_resources: On-line sources, and journal articles on theories of the origin of human beings

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of theories of the origin of human beings
activities.learning_activity: (b) Describe the stages of human evolution and their technological Ages
suggested_methods: Guide students in groups to role-play the physical development of a child from sitting, crawling to walking in relation to stages of human evolution from primates, homo erectus to homo-sapiens, including tools used and economic activities performed in each stage of human evolution
activities.assessment_criteria: The stages of human evolution and their technological ages are clearly described
suggested_resources: Charts, pictures, journal articles on the stages of human evolution and their technological Ages

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the economic, political, social and technological development of pre-colonial Africa
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe different modes of production in pre-colonial Africa
suggested_methods: Let students visit a nearby society to study ownership and characteristics of various production activities, paste each group’s findings on class walls, and let each group study other groups’ findings.
Let them relate the findings with various modes of production one after another in pre-colonial Africa
activities.assessment_criteria: Different modes of production in pre-colonial Africa are evidently described
suggested_resources: Pictures and journal articles on different modes of production in pre-colonial Africa

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the economic, political, social and technological development of pre-colonial Africa
activities.learning_activity: (b) Explain different economic activities and their impact on pre-colonial African societies
suggested_methods: Organise students to share economic activities available in the nearby society and their impacts on society. Let them relate the findings with different economic activities and their impact on pre-colonial African societies
activities.assessment_criteria: Different economic activities and their impact on pre-colonial African societies are clearly explained
suggested_resources: Journal articles and on-line sources on different economic activities and their impact on pre-colonial African societies

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the economic, political, social and technological development of pre-colonial Africa
activities.learning_activity: (c) Describe social-political organisations and education in pre-colonial African societies
suggested_methods: Organise students in manageable groups to discuss social-political organisations and education in pre-colonial African societies
activities.assessment_criteria: Social-political organisations and education in pre-colonial African societies are evidently described
suggested_resources: Charts, and pictures on social-political organisations and education in pre-colonial African societies

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of interactions among African societies during the pre-colonial period
activities.learning_activity: Describe the pre-colonial interactions among the societies of Indian Ocean Islands, Eastern, Western, Northern and Southern Africa (conditions and consequences) Each student to imagine the types, factors that facilitated interactions and effects of pre-colonial interactions with societies of Indian Ocean Islands, Eastern, Western, Northern and Southern Africa
activities.assessment_criteria: The pre-colonial interactions among the societies of Indian Ocean Islands, Eastern, Western, Northern and Southern Africa (conditions and consequences) are clearly described
suggested_resources: On-line materials and journal articles on pre-colonial interactions among the societies of Indian Ocean Islands, Eastern, Western, Northern and Southern Africa

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
activities.specific_competence: 1.2. Demonstrate an understanding of the early contacts among the people of Africa, the Middle East, the Far East and Europe from the 1st Century to the 19th Century
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe the early contacts between African societies and the Middle East, Far East and Europe from the 1st Century up to the 15th Century (conditions and consequences) Use jigsaw to let students study African contact with either the Middle East, Far East or Europe by focusing on factors that facilitated the contact and the effects from the 1st Century to the 15th Century, then let each group present in the whole class. Let them reflect on the existing African relations with the Middle East, Far East and Europe and relate them with those of the 1st Century to the 15th Century
suggested_methods: Use jigsaw
activities.assessment_criteria: The early contacts between African societies and the Middle East, Far East and Europe from the 1st Century to the 15th Century (conditions and consequences) are clearly described
suggested_resources: On-line sources and journal articles on early contacts between African societies and the Middle East, Far East and Europe from the 1st Century up to the 15th

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
activities.specific_competence: 1.2. Demonstrate an understanding of the early contacts among the people of Africa, the Middle East, the Far East and Europe from the 1st Century to the 19th Century
activities.learning_activity: (b) Explain the contacts between African societies and the Portuguese, French, Dutch and British from the 15th Century up to the 18th Century By using the jigsaw, divide students into four groups and assign each group to study African societies’ contacts with either the Portuguese or French or Dutch or the British with an emphasis on factors that facilitated the contact and the effects from the 15th Century to the 18th Century; then let each group present followed by discussions
suggested_methods: By using the jigsaw
activities.assessment_criteria: The contacts between African societies and the Portuguese, French, Dutch and the British from the 15th Century up to the 18th Century are clearly explained
suggested_resources: Journal articles and on-line sources on the contacts between African societies and the Portuguese, French, Dutch and British from the 15th Century up to the 18th Century

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of African history before colonialism
activities.specific_competence: 1.2. Demonstrate an understanding of the early contacts among the people of Africa, the Middle East, the Far East and Europe from the 1st Century to the 19th Century
activities.learning_activity: (c) Describe the origin, development and effects of the slave trade from the 15th Century to the 19th Century Use scenarios to guide students in describing the origin, development and effects of the slave trade from the 15th Century to the 19th Century
suggested_methods: Use scenarios
activities.assessment_criteria: The origin, development and effects of the slave trade from the 15th Century to the 19th Century are clearly described
suggested_resources: Photographs, charts and on-line sources on the origin, development and effects of the slave trade from the 15th Century to the 19th Century

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the development of capitalism and its impact on Africa
activities.learning_activity: Describe the development of capitalism in Europe and its impact on Africa
suggested_methods: Guide students in groups to discuss and role-play the development of capitalism in Europe and its impact on Africa
activities.assessment_criteria: The development of capitalism in Europe and its impact on Africa is clearly described
suggested_resources: Journal articles, reports, speeches and on-line sources on the development of capitalism in Europe and its impact on Africa

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of colonial systems in Africa
activities.learning_activity: (a) Discuss colonial administrative systems in Africa (German, French, British and Portuguese)
suggested_methods: Use scenario/simulation to guide students to discuss colonial administrative systems in Africa (German, French, British and Portuguese)
activities.assessment_criteria: The colonial administrative systems in Africa (German, French, British and Portuguese) are clearly discussed
suggested_resources: Photographs, charts, and on-line sources on colonial administrative systems in Africa

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of colonial systems in Africa
activities.learning_activity: (b) Discuss colonial economic systems in Africa (Establishment, characteristics, sectors, labour issues)
suggested_methods: By using ICT support learning tools, guide students to discuss colonial economic systems in Africa (Establishment, characteristics, sectors, labour issues)
activities.assessment_criteria: The colonial economic systems in Africa are accurately discussed
suggested_resources: On-line sources, and pictures on colonial economic systems in Africa

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of colonial systems in Africa
activities.learning_activity: (c) Assess the nature and patterns of colonial social services in Africa
suggested_methods: Facilitate students in four groups and assign each group one of the colonial social services (education, health, water, housing) to explore and write a report on the nature and patterns of such a service. Let them share the report with the whole class for discussions
activities.assessment_criteria: The nature and patterns of colonial social services in Africa are accurately assessed
suggested_resources: On-line sources, pictures and photographs on the nature and patterns of colonial social services in Africa

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of colonial systems in Africa
activities.learning_activity: (d) Relate colonial economic, political, infrastructure, and education systems in Africa with the current African systems
suggested_methods: Guide students to research on the relationship between the colonial economic, political, infrastructure, and education systems in Africa with the current African systems (focusing on similarities and differences)
activities.assessment_criteria: The colonial economic, political, infrastructure, and education systems in Africa are comprehensively related with the current African systems
suggested_resources: On-line sources on relationships between colonial economic, political, infrastructure, and education systems in Africa with the current African systems

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the history of nationalist movements in Africa
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe nationalist movements in Africa
suggested_methods: By using jigsaw, guide students to describe the nationalist movements in Africa, emphasising tracing the development of pre-nationalist movements (religious, peasants, welfare and workers` associations). Guide them to relate pre-nationalist movements with mass movements
activities.assessment_criteria: Nationalist movements in Africa are correctly described
suggested_resources: Photographs and on-line sources on nationalist movements in Africa

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the history of nationalist movements in Africa
activities.learning_activity: (b) Assess the rise of nationalist movements in Africa
suggested_methods: By using group discussion, guide students to assess the internal and external forces for the emergence of nationalist movements in Africa
activities.assessment_criteria: The rise of nationalist movements in Africa is accurately assessed
suggested_resources: Photographs, speeches, reports and on-line sources on the rise of nationalist movements in Africa

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the relationship between capitalism and Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Demonstrate an understanding of the history of nationalist movements in Africa
activities.learning_activity: (c) Appraise the lessons drawn from the nationalist movements in Africa
suggested_methods: In manageable groups, let students reflect on lessons that the current African countries can learn from nationalist movements
activities.assessment_criteria: The lesson drawn from the nationalist movements are appropriately reviewed

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the history of nation-building in post-colonial Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the history of nation-building in post-colonial Africa
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe the concept and origin of nation-building in Africa
suggested_methods: Guide students in groups to discuss the meaning and evolution of nation-building in various post-colonial African countries
activities.assessment_criteria: The concept and origin of nation – building in Africa is well described
suggested_resources: On-line sources
journal articles
reports
speeches on the concept and origin of nation-building in Africa

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the history of nation-building in post-colonial Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the history of nation-building in post-colonial Africa
activities.learning_activity: (b) Evaluate the strategies used in nation-building
suggested_methods: Invite a guest speaker to present on the strategies used in nations-building in Africa
activities.assessment_criteria: The strategies used in nation building in Africa are precisely described

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the history of nation-building in post-colonial Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of the successes and challenges of nation-building in post-colonial Africa
activities.learning_activity: (a) Appraise the achievements and challenges of nation-building in post-colonial Africa
suggested_methods: Prepare a class debate on achievements and challenges of Nation- building in independent African countries. After the debate let the class weigh the views and finally appraise nation-building in Africa
activities.assessment_criteria: The achievements and challenges of nation-building in post-colonial Africa accurately described
suggested_resources: On-line sources
journal articles
reports and speeches on the achievements and challenges of nation-building in post-colonial Africa

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the history of nation-building in post-colonial Africa
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of the successes and challenges of nation-building in post-colonial Africa
activities.learning_activity: (b) Design and carry out a project on nation-building between the 1960s and 1970s (use one African country as a case study)
suggested_methods: Facilitate students to explore a topic for a project on nation-building between the 1960s and 1970s from one of the African countries.
Guide them to design and develop data collection tools; let them collect information, analyse, synthesise and share it before writing and submitting a final report
activities.assessment_criteria: A project on nation-building between the 1960s and 1970s by using one African country as a case study is accurately designed, carried out, and a report is submitted

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of the history of Africa’s participation in regional and international affairs
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the regional integration in Africa
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe the origin and development of the Organisation of African Unity (OAU/ African Union (AU) and economic regional groupings in Africa
suggested_methods: Divide students into four groups and assign them with appropriate references from the internet to study on the establishment, objectives, success and challenges of regional groupings (OAU/AU, EAC, SADC & ECOWAS, Arab Maghreb Union)
activities.assessment_criteria: The origin and development of the Organisation of African Unity (OAU/African Union (AU) and economic regional groupings in Africa is precisely described
suggested_resources: On-line sources
reports speeches
pictures and photographs on origin and development of the Organisation of African Unity (OAU/African Union (AU) and economic regional groupings in Africa

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of the history of Africa’s participation in regional and international affairs
activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the regional integration in Africa
activities.learning_activity: (b) Assess the performance of continental and regional integration efforts in Africa
suggested_methods: Prepare a motion on the performance of continental and regional integration efforts in Africa for students to debate. Let them write a report and share the summary in the class
activities.assessment_criteria: The performance of continental and regional integration efforts in Africa is evidently assessed
suggested_resources: On-line sources
reports speeches
pictures and photographs on the performance of continental and regional integration efforts in Africa

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of the history of Africa’s participation in regional and international affairs
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Demonstrate an understanding of the history of Africa’s participation in the United Nations Organisation
activities.learning_activity: Appraise African participation in the United Nations and its agencies
suggested_methods: Let students debate on participation of Africa in the United Nations and its agencies (UNICEF, UNDP, WHO, UNESCO, UNCHR, FAO)
activities.assessment_criteria: African participation in the United Nations and its agencies is clearly reviewed
suggested_resources: On-line sources
reports speeches
pictures and photographs on the performance of African participation in the United Nations and its agencies
